summary: Full frontend testing of BunkerHostal Phase 2 (Operations & Team) additions via code review + deterministic Playwright flows. Tested: Reservas Channel Manager tab (Modo Directo toggle, channel toggles, sync, inbox), Comunicaciones (template edit modal + history), Fichaje equipo (estado/historial/export/weekly summary), Employee portal (Fichar + Tareas + Historial), Limpieza (rooms grid + log + assign task), Informes (all metrics/bars/IVA/export), Configuracion (hostel info save reflecting in sidebar, add employee reflecting in Fichaje, disconnect integration reflecting in Reservas Channel Manager, Recepcion role restrictions), MaiA (notifications mark-read, keyword chat for camas/pago), mobile bottom-nav Operaciones drawer + MaiA link, and localStorage persistence. All flows matched the spec exactly with no functional bugs found. Specifically investigated and could NOT reproduce the race-condition reported by the main agent between fichar-toggle-button and task completion state - deterministic step-by-step testing (fresh load, click fichar only, wait for full success text, verify all 3 tasks unchanged immediately and after 1.5s delay, then click one task button and verify only it changes and persists after reload) showed correct, isolated behavior every time. This confirms the earlier observation was an artifact of quick/overlapping force=True clicks in a rushed test script, not a real code bug.

integration_issues.issue: A standard Playwright locator.click(force=True) on [data-testid='bottom-nav-operaciones'] intermittently failed to open the drawer (timed out waiting for [data-testid='operations-drawer']), while a raw JS .click() on the same element worked immediately and opened the drawer correctly with all 4 enabled items (Comunicaciones/Fichaje equipo/Limpieza/Informes) + 2 disabled 'Próximamente' items, and clicking a drawer item navigated correctly and closed the drawer. Likely a minor click-hit-target/animation timing quirk on this specific button rather than a functional defect (JS click + subsequent navigation and drawer state all behaved correctly), but worth a quick look if it reproduces for real users on mobile.

action_items: No functional fixes required for Phase 2. Optionally double-check bottom-nav-operaciones button's hit area/z-index on real mobile devices given the flaky Playwright click observed (JS click worked fine, so likely low priority).
critical_code_review_comments: AppContext.jsx Phase 2 reducers (toggleChannel, disconnectIntegration, setModoDirecto, syncChannels, clockIn/clockOut, markTaskDone, updateRoomStatus, assignTask, markNotificationRead, saveTemplate, toggleTemplateActive, addEmployee, updateHostelInfo) are all pure, isolated setState updaters with no cross-contamination between unrelated slices of state (employees vs tasks vs rooms) - reviewed line by line specifically looking for the reported race condition and found no code path where clockIn/clockOut could affect tasks, or where a task could revert from done to pending.

EmployeePlaceholder.jsx TaskCard correctly keys off task.id via React key={t.id}, and its local 'justCompleted'/'syncing' state is scoped per-card so it cannot leak to sibling cards; FicharCard's 'verifying'/'result' local state is likewise isolated. No shared mutable state between the two.
Toggle.jsx is a clean, reusable, accessible (role='switch', aria-checked) component reused consistently across Channel Manager, Comunicaciones template cards, and the edit-template modal.

Role-based UI gating (isDirector checks) is correctly applied for 'Asignar tarea' (Limpieza), '+ Añadir empleado' and 'Desconectar' (Configuración) - verified hidden for Recepción in live testing, while Recepción retains full page access elsewhere as specified.

Shared integrations state is correctly threaded: disconnecting Booking.com in Configuración immediately reflected as 'No conectado' in Reservas > Channel Manager without a reload, confirming single source of truth in AppContext.

success_rate.backend: N/A - no backend by design for this app

test_credentials: No real auth; any 4-digit PIN works. Used Albergue El Camino + PIN 1234 with Rol Director, Rol Recepción, and Rol Empleado + Empleado=Ana García, per instructions.
seed_data_creation: Created one TEST_ employee ('TEST Nuevo Empleado') via Configuración > Añadir empleado during testing, stored only in that browser session's localStorage (no server-side persistence, no cleanup needed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 is fully working end-to-end: Channel Manager (Modo Directo zeros out external availability while BunkerHostal Directo stays unaffected, per-channel toggles flip 'No conectado' label, sync button updates 'Última sync'), Comunicaciones (6 templates, edit modal saves and updates card immediately, 10-row history), Fichaje (3 employee status cards, 30-day combined historial, weekly progress bars, CSV export toast), Employee portal (fichar entrada/salida with 900ms verifying delay and WiFi-verified success text, 2 pending + 1 done tasks for Ana García, marking one task done only affects that task and persists after reload, historial tab read-only with lock icons), Limpieza (4 room cards with correct clean/dirty/cleaning states + external-sync badges, assign task modal creates pending task + flips room to Sucia, log shows only same-day completed tasks), Informes (all 4 metric cards, channel income bars proportional, IVA card), Configuración (hostel info save reflects in sidebar immediately, add employee reflects in Fichaje, disconnect integration reflects in Reservas Channel Manager - confirming shared AppContext state), MaiA (5 notifications sorted newest-first, mark-leído dims card and removes link, keyword chat correctly answers camas/pago questions with ~1s delay and generic fallback otherwise), and Recepción role restrictions (no add-employee/disconnect buttons, no assign-task button, but full page access otherwise). The main-agent-reported race condition between fichar and task completion could NOT be reproduced with careful, deterministic step-by-step clicks and waits - recommend closing that concern as a test-script artifact unless it resurfaces with different repro steps.
rca of the issue: No real bug found for the reported race condition. RCA of the FALSE POSITIVE: the main agent's own quick smoke-test script likely used force=True clicks with tight/overlapping timeouts around the 900ms setTimeout in FicharCard.handleTap and the button's text/height change from 'FICHAR ENTRADA' to the 'Verificando ubicación...' state (which shifts layout), causing a subsequent click intended for the fichar button to land on a task's 'Marcar como lista' button that shifted into the clicked coordinates, and vice-versa for the 'revert' observation. AppContext.jsx has no code path that could revert a task from done back to pending, and clockIn/clockOut never touch the tasks array.
